title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics · Execution Trace: Feed Go Count Reconciliation
summary: Reading an entry mutates global source state, bulk operations partially apply silently, device updates overwrite each other, tags leak across tenants, and unread counts drift. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go count reconciliation increment in consistent personal reader-state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.
Use versions or timestamps deliberately for multi-device conflicts and distinguish stale writes from repeated commands.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Count Reconciliation · Theory Feed Go Count Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6271 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive or maintain unread and saved counts with transaction, cache, repair, and changed-case evidence.
